component.name: ResponsiveAlertDialog
component.description: Adaptive alert dialog that renders a blocking AlertDialog on desktop (≥ 768px) and a non-dismissible vaul Drawer on mobile, preserving the destructive-action safety contract on all screen sizes.

useCases: Confirming destructive or irreversible actions across all screen sizes
Delete, remove, disconnect, or deactivate confirmations in responsive layouts
Two-step safety flows where the user must explicitly confirm before proceeding

commonPatterns.name: delete-confirmation
commonPatterns.description: Standard destructive confirmation with cancel and delete buttons
commonPatterns.composition:
<ResponsiveAlertDialog>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger asChild><Button variant="destructive">Delete Item</Button></ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>Are you sure?</ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete the item.</ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>Cancel</ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogAction variant="destructive" onClick={handleDelete}>Delete</ResponsiveAlertDialogAction>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
  </ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
</ResponsiveAlertDialog>

commonPatterns.name: with-media-icon
commonPatterns.description: Alert dialog with a media icon for visual context — icon renders identically on both surfaces
commonPatterns.composition:
<ResponsiveAlertDialog>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger asChild><Button variant="outline">Disconnect</Button></ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogMedia><Unplug /></ResponsiveAlertDialogMedia>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>Disconnect account?</ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>You will need to re-authenticate to reconnect.</ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>Keep Connected</ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogAction variant="destructive" onClick={handleDisconnect}>Disconnect</ResponsiveAlertDialogAction>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
  </ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
</ResponsiveAlertDialog>

commonPatterns.name: non-destructive-confirmation
commonPatterns.description: Primary action confirmation (not destructive) — uses default variant on action button
commonPatterns.composition:
<ResponsiveAlertDialog>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger asChild><Button variant="outline">Publish</Button></ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger>
  <ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>Publish changes?</ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>This will make your changes visible to all users.</ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader>
    <ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>Cancel</ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel>
      <ResponsiveAlertDialogAction onClick={handlePublish}>Publish</ResponsiveAlertDialogAction>
    </ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter>
  </ResponsiveAlertDialogContent>
</ResponsiveAlertDialog>

antiPatterns.reason: ResponsiveAlertDialog blocks dismissal and forces explicit acknowledgment — reserved for actions that cannot be undone
antiPatterns.alternative: Use ResponsiveDialog for non-destructive modals, forms, or detail views

antiPatterns.scenario: Using ResponsiveAlertDialog for forms or complex input
antiPatterns.reason: ResponsiveAlertDialog is designed for simple confirm/cancel flows, not multi-field forms
antiPatterns.alternative: Use ResponsiveDialog or a full-page route for forms

antiPatterns.reason: Users must always have a way to back out of destructive actions without consequences

size.note: Size applies to AlertDialogContent on desktop only. The mobile Drawer always uses natural content height.

mobileVariant.note: ResponsiveAlertDialog always uses Drawer on mobile — there is no sheet option. The Drawer is rendered with dismissible=false and showHandle=false to prevent accidental dismissal and preserve the safety contract.

slots.trigger: ResponsiveAlertDialogTrigger — routes to AlertDialogTrigger (desktop) or DrawerTrigger (mobile)
slots.content: ResponsiveAlertDialogContent — routes to AlertDialogContent (desktop) or DrawerContent with showHandle=false (mobile)
slots.header: ResponsiveAlertDialogHeader — routes to AlertDialogHeader (desktop) or DrawerHeader (mobile)
slots.media: ResponsiveAlertDialogMedia — icon or image slot in the header; renders AlertDialogMedia on both surfaces
slots.title: ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle — routes to AlertDialogTitle (desktop) or DrawerTitle (mobile)
slots.description: ResponsiveAlertDialogDescription — routes to AlertDialogDescription (desktop) or DrawerDescription (mobile)
slots.footer: ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter — routes to AlertDialogFooter (desktop) or DrawerFooter (mobile)
slots.action: ResponsiveAlertDialogAction — confirm button; closes the surface on click. Renders AlertDialogAction on desktop, Button + DrawerClose on mobile. Defaults to variant='primary'; use variant='destructive' for irreversible actions.
slots.cancel: ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel — always outline; closes the surface without executing the action. Renders AlertDialogCancel on desktop, Button variant=outline + DrawerClose on mobile.

parentConstraints: All ResponsiveAlertDialog* sub-components must be descendants of ResponsiveAlertDialog
ResponsiveAlertDialogAction and ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel must be inside ResponsiveAlertDialogFooter
ResponsiveAlertDialogTitle must be inside ResponsiveAlertDialogContent for accessibility

interactions.desktop-open: AlertDialog overlay fades in with backdrop blur; content zooms in with fade
interactions.desktop-close: Escape or cancel/action buttons close the dialog. Cannot be dismissed by clicking outside.
interactions.mobile-open: Drawer slides up from bottom; no drag handle shown
interactions.mobile-close: Only action or cancel buttons can close the drawer. Cannot be dragged away or dismissed by tapping the overlay (dismissible=false). This preserves the safety contract on mobile.
interactions.action: Executes the confirmed operation and closes the surface. Attach side-effects via onClick.
interactions.cancel: Closes the surface without executing any operation

responsive.desktop: ≥ 768px: blocking AlertDialog; cannot be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape without explicit action

accessibility.role: alertdialog on desktop (Radix AlertDialog); dialog on mobile (vaul Drawer)
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab cycles focus within content; Enter/Space activates buttons. Escape is intentionally blocked on mobile via dismissible=false to prevent accidental dismissal of destructive confirmation.
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as an alert dialog on desktop; title and description associated via aria attributes; focus trapped within open surface
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus trapped inside the open surface; returns to trigger element on close

rules: Use ResponsiveAlertDialog instead of AlertDialog whenever the UI must work on both desktop and mobile.
Always include both ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel and ResponsiveAlertDialogAction — never omit cancel.
Use variant='destructive' on ResponsiveAlertDialogAction for irreversible actions (delete, remove, disconnect).
Use variant='primary' (default) on ResponsiveAlertDialogAction for critical but non-destructive confirmations.
ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel always renders as outline — do not override its variant.
ResponsiveAlertDialogAction defaults to size='sm'; ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel defaults to size='sm'.
The mobile Drawer is always non-dismissible (dismissible=false, showHandle=false) — this is intentional and must not be overridden.
There is no sheet mobileVariant — ResponsiveAlertDialog only uses Drawer on mobile.
ResponsiveAlertDialogMedia renders identically on both surfaces; it is the only slot with no surface-specific behavior.

aiHints.context: Use ResponsiveAlertDialog as the responsive replacement for AlertDialog — any time a destructive or irreversible action needs confirmation and the UI must work on mobile. On desktop it renders a blocking AlertDialog; on mobile it renders a non-dismissible vaul Drawer (dismissible=false, showHandle=false) so the user cannot accidentally swipe away a delete confirmation. Always pair ResponsiveAlertDialogAction (variant='destructive' for irreversible, variant='primary' otherwise) with ResponsiveAlertDialogCancel.
